questions.q: In Hindu thought, the term 'Shakti' primarily refers to which of the following?
options: A sacred mountain in the Himalayas
The divine feminine cosmic energy or power
A river that flows from heaven
questions.explanation: Shakti denotes the dynamic feminine energy or power through which the universe is created, sustained, and dissolved.

questions.q: In the Shaktism tradition, Shakti is conceived as inseparable from which great male deity?
questions.explanation: Shaktism conceives the dynamic feminine Shakti as inseparable from the static masculine principle, identified with Shiva.

questions.q: The devotional tradition that venerates Shakti as the supreme reality is known by which name?
questions.explanation: Shaktism is the tradition that worships the Goddess as Shakti, the supreme reality from whom all goddesses emanate.

questions.q: Which celebrated text narrates the Goddess slaying the buffalo demon and is a central scripture of Shakti worship?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya, part of the Markandeya Purana, is a foundational text of Shaktism celebrating the Goddess's victories.

questions.q: In Shaktism, Shakti is exalted as the primordial supreme power known by which title?
questions.explanation: Shaktism venerates Shakti as Adi Parashakti, the primordial supreme power from whom all goddesses emanate.

questions.q: Shakti is most often described in Hindu tradition as embodying which aspect of divinity?
questions.explanation: Shakti embodies the feminine creative energy through which the cosmos comes into being and operates.

questions.q: According to Shaktism, the many goddesses of the Hindu pantheon are understood to be what in relation to Shakti?
options: Emanations or forms of the one Shakti
questions.explanation: In Shaktism all goddesses are regarded as emanations or forms of the one supreme Shakti, the Adi Parashakti.

questions.q: In Hindu cosmology, Shakti is said to be the power behind which three great cosmic functions?
options: Creation, sustenance, and dissolution of the universe
Birth, marriage, and death of mortals
questions.explanation: Shakti is the dynamic energy through which the universe is created, sustained, and ultimately dissolved.

questions.q: The fierce warrior goddess born to slay demons the gods could not defeat, regarded as a form of Shakti, is known as whom?
questions.explanation: Durga, the invincible warrior goddess of the Devi Mahatmya, is a celebrated form of the supreme Shakti.

questions.q: Which Purana, devoted to the Goddess, is a principal scripture of the Shakta tradition alongside the Devi Mahatmya?
questions.explanation: The Devi Bhagavata Purana is a major Shakta scripture that, with the Devi Mahatmya, exalts the Goddess as supreme.

questions.q: The Devi Mahatmya, the core text glorifying Shakti, is traditionally regarded as a section of which larger Purana?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya is embedded within the Markandeya Purana, where it celebrates the Goddess's exploits.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the Goddess as Durga is most famous for slaying which buffalo-shaped demon?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya recounts the Goddess slaying Mahishasura, the buffalo demon, earning her the name Mahishasuramardini.

questions.q: The androgynous form uniting Shiva and his Shakti in a single body is known in the Puranas by which name?
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara, the half-male, half-female form, expresses the inseparable union of Shiva and his Shakti.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the goddess Kali is said to emerge from the brow of which form of the Great Goddess?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya describes Kali springing forth from the wrathful brow of Ambika (Durga) to destroy the demons.

questions.q: According to the Devi Mahatmya, the demons Shumbha and Nishumbha were ultimately slain by which goddess, a form of Shakti?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya narrates the Great Goddess defeating the demon brothers Shumbha and Nishumbha and their hordes.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the demons Madhu and Kaitabha are destroyed through the power of the Goddess in which aspect that overcame Vishnu's sleep?
questions.explanation: The Goddess as Yoganidra, the cosmic sleep upon Vishnu, withdraws so he can wake and slay Madhu and Kaitabha.

questions.q: In Shakta theology, Shiva without his Shakti is famously described in what way to stress her vital power?
options: As the brightest of all suns
As inert or lifeless (like a corpse)
As the swiftest of the gods
questions.explanation: Shakta teaching holds that Shiva without Shakti is inert as a corpse, emphasizing her as the active animating power.

questions.q: The autumn festival of nine nights dedicated to the worship of the Goddess as Shakti is known by which name?
questions.explanation: Navaratri, the nine-night festival, is devoted to the worship of the Goddess in her many forms as Shakti.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the combined radiance of the assembled gods gives rise to which mighty form of Shakti?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya describes the gods' combined energies coalescing into Durga to defeat Mahishasura.

questions.q: In the Shaktism tradition, which famous text describing the Goddess's thousand names is widely recited in her honor?
questions.explanation: The Lalita Sahasranama enumerates a thousand names of the Goddess and is a beloved Shakta devotional text.

questions.q: In Tantric and Shakta tradition, Shakti is often visualized as a coiled energy resting at the base of the spine, known as what?
questions.explanation: Kundalini is the coiled Shakti said to rest at the base of the spine, rising through the body's centers in yogic practice.

questions.q: The sites where parts of the goddess Sati's body are said to have fallen, sacred to Shakti worship, are called by which name?
questions.explanation: The Shakti Pithas are pilgrimage shrines marking where parts of Sati's body fell, each venerated as a seat of the Goddess.

questions.q: The Devi Mahatmya is structured around the praise of the Goddess in which three principal forms, recited across its chapters?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya glorifies the Goddess as Mahakali, Mahalakshmi, and Mahasaraswati across its three great episodes.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the demon Raktabija posed a unique threat because of which power, requiring Kali's intervention?
options: Every drop of his blood that fell became a new demon
He could become invisible at will
He could not be harmed by any weapon
He could fly faster than thought
questions.explanation: Raktabija multiplied from every drop of his spilled blood, so Kali drank his blood before it touched the ground.

questions.q: In Shakta and Tantric tradition, the group of fierce mother-goddesses who are emanations of the Great Goddess's power is collectively known as what?
questions.explanation: The Matrikas, or Saptamatrikas, are the mother-goddesses who issue as energies of the gods and of the Great Goddess in battle.

questions.q: The sacred geometric diagram central to the worship of Shakti as Lalita Tripurasundari is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Sri Yantra, or Sri Chakra, is the geometric form through which the Goddess Lalita Tripurasundari is worshipped.

questions.q: In Shakta Tantra, the ten great goddesses representing aspects of the supreme Shakti are collectively called by which name?
questions.explanation: The Mahavidyas, the ten great wisdom-goddesses including Kali and Tara, embody facets of the supreme Shakti in Shakta Tantra.

questions.q: In the philosophy of Kashmir Shaivism and Shakta thought, the term used for the Goddess's power that manifests the cosmos through creative play is best described as what?
options: Maya as her creative power (Shakti's manifestation)
questions.explanation: Shakti is understood as the creative power, often termed Maya, by which the supreme reality projects and unfolds the cosmos.

questions.q: The Devi Mahatmya is also widely known and recited under which alternative title?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya is also called the Durga Saptashati or Chandi, named for its roughly seven hundred verses to the Goddess.

questions.q: In Shakta theology, the Goddess is identified with the syllable and concept central to the Devi Mahatmya's teaching that she is identical with the supreme reality, namely what?
questions.explanation: Shakta theology identifies the Goddess with Brahman, the absolute reality, affirming Shakti as the supreme ground of all existence.
